title: pelargonium × venosum
intro: Pelargonium × venosum, also known as Cape Rose or Red Stork's Bill, is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae. It is native to South Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including relieving symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to soothe sore throats and calm the nervous system. Herbal preparations of Pelargonium × venosum, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used to promote respiratory health and support immune function.
